name: GEEKOM IT12 Mini PC (Intel i5-12450H)
shortDescription: The GEEKOM IT12 is a business-grade mini PC with Intel Core i5-12450H and Intel Iris Xe Graphics, running local 7B–13B LLMs via Ollama. With 16GB DDR4, a 3-year warranty, and WiFi 6E, it is one of the best-built compact AI inference machines under $400 in 2026.

specs.chip: Intel Core i5-12450H (12th Gen Alder Lake)

useCases: Local LLM inference via Ollama (7B–13B models)
Intel Iris Xe GPU-accelerated AI with OpenVINO
Business desktop replacement with AI capabilities
pros: 3-year warranty — best-in-class support for a mini PC
Intel Iris Xe with 96 EUs — faster iGPU than N95 or basic UHD for AI
16GB DDR4 supports 7B at full precision, 13B at Q4
WiFi 6E and 2.5Gbps Ethernet — ideal for a home AI server
Upgradeable RAM and storage via SO-DIMM and M.2 slots
cons: Iris Xe iGPU is too slow for Stable Diffusion at usable quality
51 GB/s bandwidth limits LLM inference vs Apple Silicon
Intel OpenVINO required for iGPU acceleration — not plug-and-play
Windows only — no native macOS Ollama Metal acceleration
verdict: The GEEKOM IT12 earns its reputation as a reliable business mini PC that moonlights as a local AI node. The i5-12450H handles 7B models smoothly at 8–15 tokens/second via CPU, and Intel Iris Xe can accelerate OpenVINO-compatible models. The 3-year warranty sets it apart from budget brands. For a dependable, always-on AI assistant that also handles everyday computing, the IT12 is an excellent choice.

faq.question: Can the GEEKOM IT12 run local LLMs?
faq.answer: Yes. Using Ollama on Windows 11 Pro, the IT12 runs Llama 3.1 8B at approximately 8–15 tokens/second via the i5-12450H CPU. For OpenVINO-optimized models, the Intel Iris Xe iGPU can push this higher. 13B Q4 models run at 4–8 tokens/second. Not fast by GPU standards, but reliable and consistent.

faq.question: What makes the GEEKOM IT12 different from other budget mini PCs?
faq.answer: The 3-year warranty is the key differentiator. Most budget mini PC brands offer 1-year coverage; GEEKOM backs the IT12 for 3 years with US-based support. The i5-12450H also outperforms entry-level processors like the N95 or Ryzen 4300U, making it a better choice for workloads that run continuously.

faq.question: Can the GEEKOM IT12 run Stable Diffusion?
faq.answer: Technically yes, but slowly. The Intel Iris Xe iGPU with DirectML on Windows can run SD 1.5 at 512×512, but expect 30–60 seconds per image. SDXL is impractical. For image generation, a dedicated GPU is strongly recommended.

notFor: Anyone who wants fast LLM responses — CPU inference at 12 t/s feels sluggish for interactive chat
Stable Diffusion — no discrete GPU means SDXL takes minutes per image
Running 13B+ models — 32 GB RAM is sufficient but CPU speed is the bottleneck
Power users who benchmark hardware — this is a convenience buy, not a performance buy
entityHeadline: Running Ollama on the GEEKOM IT12: Always-On Local AI Without a GPU
